title: Presentation readers show the session's lane (preamble, status, lifecycle hooks)
action: Migrate the PRESENTATION readers to lane resolution (per D4, zero-lane byte-parity). LIB CAPABILITY where the owning hook is out of scope (validation repair W-4): (1) inject.buildSessionPreamble / buildPromptReminder gain an OPTIONAL sessionId parameter defaulting to today's exact output; when a bound sessionId is provided, the preamble shows THAT lane's phase/mode/feature/gates plus a one-line lanes summary (N other active lanes and their features) when other lanes exist. Proven by direct test_lib.mjs calls; the SessionStart hook threading is DEFERRED to S4 (which owns bee-session-init for the rehydrate branch) — do not touch bee-session-init.mjs. ANCHOR CORRECTION (cold-pickup repair): the pinned preamble rows live where grep 'buildSessionPreamble' lands in test_lib.mjs (currently ~:949-1224, NOT :746-887 which is now claims/race/guard tests after S1) — those rows must pass unmodified for the no-sessionId call. (2) bee.mjs buildStatus — add a lanes block (per-lane phase/gates/binding) alongside the existing single-pipeline fields, which keep their exact zero-lane shape; bee_status.mjs is a thin shim over the same buildStatus so bee-vs-shim parity is structural. Status rows: test_lib.mjs spawns bee_status.mjs --json and inspects the payload (grep bee_status.mjs in test_lib.mjs) — add lanes-block rows there. (3) hooks/bee-chain-nudge.mjs + hooks/bee-session-close.mjs — both already parse ctx.payload: thread payload.session_id through so the phase they consult comes from the acting session's pipeline via resolvePipeline, default fallback; their zero-lane hook-contract rows (route-stop-handler + live-state-untouched hash rows, grep those names in hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs) pass unmodified, with new rows for the bound case. Migration extends coverage, never weakens. Vendor byte-identical siblings under .bee/bin/.

truths: Zero-lane repos render today's preamble, status output, and hook behavior byte-identically (pinned rows pass unmodified; no-sessionId calls are byte-identical)
buildSessionPreamble with a bound sessionId shows that lane's phase/gates and names other active lanes in one line (direct lib call)
Status output carries a lanes block while every pre-existing field keeps its exact zero-lane shape
bee-chain-nudge and bee-session-close consult the acting session's pipeline when the payload carries a bound session_id, default otherwise

artifacts.substantive: optional-sessionId preamble with lane view + lanes summary and explicit default fallback

key_links: bee-chain-nudge/bee-session-close thread payload.session_id into the resolution
prohibitions: No changes to bee-session-init.mjs (S4 owns the SessionStart threading + rehydrate branch)
No enforcement logic changes (guards/cells are fsh-5's)
No weakened or deleted existing assertion

trace.outcome: Presentation readers (preamble, status, chain-nudge/session-close) resolve through the acting session's lane (D4): buildSessionPreamble/buildPromptReminder gain an optional sessionId param (omitted = byte-identical default pipeline); a bound session renders that lane's phase/mode/feature/gates plus a one-line summary of other ACTIVE lanes, an unresolvable binding falls back to the default. bee.mjs buildStatus carries a new lanes block (per-lane phase/gates/bound sessions) alongside every pre-existing zero-lane field, reusing the fsh-4 lane-row composition (handleStateLanes refactored onto the same buildLaneRows helper, no second implementation); renderStatusText adds a Lanes line only when lanes exist. bee-chain-nudge/bee-session-close thread payload.session_id through resolvePipeline for the phase they consult on, default fallback; bee-session-init.mjs untouched (S4 scope). Vendored .bee/bin siblings byte-identical.
